text:
Her ears were bleeding. It was February 2023, at Sunset Beach on HawaiГўВҖВҷiГўВҖВҷs North Shore midway through the World Surf League Championship TourГўВҖВҷs second event of the season, when Caroline Marks woke up feeling like something was stabbing her face from the inside. Her pillow was soaked in blood. On her way to the hospital, she couldnГўВҖВҷt stop crying.

ГўВҖВңSo much pain,ГўВҖВқ she says now, during an early-January drive north on the Pacific Coast Highway from her home in San Clemente, Calif., to Salt Creek Beach in Dana Point. Her ear canals had closed and ruptured, she explains. Issues related to her deviated septum and an influx of seawater had created infections in her sinuses, adenoids and ears.

That night, the doctor ordered immediate surgery. ГўВҖВңWell,ГўВҖВқ she replied, ГўВҖВңI have to surf.ГўВҖВқ

She returned to finish the event in second place, passing off her illness to reporters as a mild ear infection. ГўВҖВңIt was so gnarly,ГўВҖВқ Marks says now, laughing. ГўВҖВңSohectic.ГўВҖВқ

SheГўВҖВҷs O.K. now. After the WSL Finals in SeptemberГўВҖВ”when she won her first world championshipГўВҖВ”Marks had surgery in October, fixing her deviated septum and removing her adenoids. ГўВҖВңIГўВҖВҷll probably still need my ears drilled soon,ГўВҖВқ she says, ГўВҖВңbut at least my nose is working right now. IГўВҖВҷve been able to breathe way better. I didnГўВҖВҷt realize I couldnГўВҖВҷt breathe that well until now.ГўВҖВқ

ThatГўВҖВҷs a fitting metaphor for the 22-year-old superstar. Afterbecoming a globally recognized phenom as young as 13, Marks endured an experience all too familiar for celebrity wunderkinds. She found herselfГўВҖВ”and her surfingГўВҖВ”suffocating under the weight of the worldГўВҖВҷs expectations. What she did over the past year wasnГўВҖВҷt just an incredible performance. It was a reclamation of herself.

About that performance. After forgoing surgery in February, Marks surfed through the next eight months of the season, winning three times and finishing in the top five in eight of nine events. She qualified forthe Paris Olympicsand won that world title, fulfilling what had long seemed to be her surfing destiny. Marks has dominated the sport at every level since she was 11. At 13, she became the youngest surfer to appear in a WSL Champions Tour event. In 2021, at 19, she was the youngest surfer in the Olympics. ГўВҖВңThe potential has been there for years,ГўВҖВқ 11-time WSL champion Kelly Slater said in September. ГўВҖВңItГўВҖВҷs great to see it come together.ГўВҖВқ

The world title came after a year when MarksГўВҖВҷs surfing future seemed in doubt: She stepped away from the tour for several months in 2022 to do a full reset for her mind and her life, leaving California for some much-needed time with family in her home state of Florida. ГўВҖВңEvery moment away from the tour felt like an eternity,ГўВҖВқ she says. ГўВҖВңBut that made me really focus on what I needed to focus on, and not run away from it.ГўВҖВқ

Born on ValentineГўВҖВҷs Day 2002 in Boca Raton, Fla., Marks grew up in Melbourne Beach, on FloridaГўВҖВҷs east coast. Darren and Sarah Marks already had three sons; CarolineГўВҖВҷs arrival made Sarah cry in joy. ГўВҖВңOh yay,ГўВҖВқ she recalls thinking. ГўВҖВңWe can do girly stuff!ГўВҖВқ Sarah loved horses and introduced Caroline early. ГўВҖВңBraids and pink boots,ГўВҖВқ she says. By the age of 7, Caroline excelled at barrel racing. ГўВҖВңShe had no fear,ГўВҖВқ says her brother Luke, three years her senior.

The Marks were an active bunch. Surfing, horses, motocross, skateboardingГўВҖВ”they had a full halfpipe along with a dirt track right in their backyard. And Caroline had energy to burn. When Sarah went for walks on the beach, Caroline ГўВҖВңran laps around me,ГўВҖВқ Sarah says, ГўВҖВңfor miles and miles.ГўВҖВқ

At 8, CarolineГўВҖВҷs love of horses gave way to passion for the waves. ГўВҖВңAll she would say,ГўВҖВқ recalls Sarah, ГўВҖВңis, ГўВҖВҳI wanna be with my brothers, I wanna be with my brothers!ГўВҖВҷ ГўВҖВқ

Luke, the familyГўВҖВҷs best surfer, gave her an old board. They took her out on a Friday afternoon. She caught waves by the end of Day 1, riding whatГўВҖВҷs known as goofy-footed, right foot forward instead of the traditional left. ГўВҖВңJust felt better,ГўВҖВқ she says. ГўВҖВңI went with it.ГўВҖВқ

By Saturday she cruised, riding waves with ease. By Monday, she carved, making sharp, controlled turns. Every day afterward, she crossed the backyard for the beach and waves beyond. She hung posters featuring surfers likeCarissa Moore, the Hawaiian who won her first world title in 2011 at age 18, when Caroline was 9. When her grandfather offered to buy her a horse, she declined, saying she just wanted to surf now.

Soon Caroline didnГўВҖВҷt just surf with her brothers; she tried to beat them. Some sessions ended in tears, but she always went back. ГўВҖВңThey were hard on me, but it came from love,ГўВҖВқ she says. ГўВҖВңThey saw something in me. And I just wanted to impress them, make them gowhoa!ГўВҖВқ

In her very first competitive event, a neighborhood contest, 9-year-old Caroline beat everyone her age, boys and girls. She finished fourth the event after that, a result shehated so much she wanted to quit. ГўВҖВңYou canГўВҖВҷt quit,ГўВҖВқ Luke told her. ГўВҖВңYouГўВҖВҷre too good.ГўВҖВқ

She didnГўВҖВҷt quit, but after that she trashed any trophy that wasnГўВҖВҷt first place. During competitions, she surfed even while waiting for her heats, catching every wave she could. ГўВҖВңWe have a term for surfers like that,ГўВҖВқ says Darren, a longtime amateur surfer himself. ГўВҖВңTheyГўВҖВҷre ГўВҖВҳfrothing.ГўВҖВҷ ГўВҖВқ They canГўВҖВҷt get enough.

At age 11, Caroline put herself on the surf world map. She swept the U-12, U-14 and U-16 divisions at the Atlantic Surfing Federation championships at Sebastian Inlet, Fla., where she also caught her first barrel. ГўВҖВңThe ultimate goal as a surfer,ГўВҖВқ she says. It takes most surfers years to catch one. Team USA signed her within a week, its younger surfer ever. The following year, at the National Scholastic Surfing Association championships in Huntington Beach, Calif.ГўВҖВ”the countryГўВҖВҷs largest amateur competitionГўВҖВ”Caroline won the all-ages womenГўВҖВҷs Open contest. Surfing in a powerful squat, with strong legs and a low center of gravity, she overshadowed the best amateurs in the country and carved the tops off CaliforniaГўВҖВҷs larger waves with ease, even at her young age. ГўВҖВңHer feet seemed stuck to the board,ГўВҖВқ says retired pro surfer Mike Parsons. ГўВҖВңNothing could knock her off her board.ГўВҖВқ

Parsons soon became her coach and agent. Over the next few years, Caroline ripped through amateur competition, winning more than a dozen national and international titles across a slew of ages and divisions. Along the way, at 13, she became the youngest surfer to make the WSL Champions Tour, the sportГўВҖВҷs highest level of competition, after receiving a wild-card placement at Lower Trestles, a breathtaking and iconic location with a gorgeous beach and waves that break long and high and smooth. Suddenly Caroline found herself in a heat against Moore, the hero from her posters. And she held her own.

ГўВҖВңShe was this radical little spark plug of a power surfer,ГўВҖВқ says Matt Biolos, founder of the Lost surfboard company and apparel brand and MarksГўВҖВҷs exclusive board maker since she went pro. ГўВҖВңSheГўВҖВҷs like a Simone Biles. Whatever she tells her body to do, it does it well.ГўВҖВқ

Most of her competitors used boards with thinner, curved rockers to help them gain speed and power on the wave. Caroline was so strong she needed the opposite; Biolos made her boards increasingly thicker and nearly flat. ГўВҖВңShe needs the resistance,ГўВҖВқ he says.

By 16, Caroline made the tour full-time and soon became known as its happiest surfer. Cold and rainy conditions would leave everyone miserableГўВҖВ”except Caroline, whoГўВҖВҷd smile and laugh, going, ГўВҖВңIsnГўВҖВҷt this great, you guys? IsnГўВҖВҷt this great?ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңShe just drove everyone nuts,ГўВҖВқ Parsons says. ГўВҖВңThen sheГўВҖВҷd hit the water and go full assassin mode.ГўВҖВқ

Marks finished her first full season ranked No. 7 in the world and was named WSL Rookie of the Year in 2018. In the season opener the following year, she defeated Moore in the final heat to claim her first career WSL win. At 17, she became the No. 1ГўВҖВ“ranked surfer in the world.

Now everyone noticed her. Her Instagram following skyrocketed. When she casually went surfing in San Clemente, people knew her in the water. Most of it was good. But some of it wasnГўВҖВҷt. And what wasnГўВҖВҷt good got to her.

ГўВҖВңYou have people that love you, and you have people that hate you,ГўВҖВқ Marks says. ГўВҖВңAnd you have people that think you look good, and you know, people that think you donГўВҖВҷt look good. And thatГўВҖВҷs hard, because to me, there could be 99 positive comments, but itГўВҖВҷs so easy to fixate on the negative one.ГўВҖВқ

Then there was Instagram. ГўВҖВңAnyone in the world can get into your phone now, right in your pocket,ГўВҖВқ Caroline says. ГўВҖВңWhich can be amazing! But sometimes ГўВҖВҰГўВҖВқ She laughs. ГўВҖВңSometimes, really not good! You just do your best to block it out. It was all so new to me and it was shocking, like, people can really be this way? ItГўВҖВҷs like, you know you should just ignore them, but itГўВҖВҷs hard.ГўВҖВқ

Marks quit reading social media comments, and, when she turned 18, she posed for the Sports Illustrated Swimsuit Issue, trying to kill her fears by embracing them. She moved into a home she purchased in the hills overlooking San Clemente, claiming a sense of strength from the independence of living alone for the first time.

Still, breaking free of the expectations that came with stardom was difficult. With people constantly telling her how great she would be or should be, she says, ГўВҖВңI started to feel like I was letting people down if I lost.ГўВҖВқ

She just trained and surfed, obsessed. No parties, no prom, not even nice restaurants on the road. ГўВҖВңIt was just the hotel to the beach and back,ГўВҖВқ she says.

Marks finished 2019 as No. 2 in the world, just behind Moore, and made the ГўВҖВҷ20 Tokyo Olympics. When COVID-19 hit and postponed the Games to ГўВҖВҷ21, Marks coped by training twice a day. At the Olympics, she recorded the highest score of any surfer there, male or female. But two rounds later she lost, placing fourth and failing to medal.

ГўВҖВңThat was rough,ГўВҖВқ she says. She began breaking down. Smiled less, stressed more, doubted herself like never before, didnГўВҖВҷt feel good in the water, and spent less time in it. ГўВҖВңI just wasnГўВҖВҷt having fun,ГўВҖВқ Marks says. ГўВҖВңAnd then I was feeling guilty about that. ThatГўВҖВҷs where I really struggled, too. I was like,Why do I feel like this when I have this dream life?ГўВҖВқ

Marks had long conversations with her family about stepping away. ГўВҖВңIt was really confusing,ГўВҖВқ she says. ГўВҖВңLike, ГўВҖВҳWhatГўВҖВҷs going on? Why donГўВҖВҷt I feel like I normally do?ГўВҖВҷ I was just lost in the sauce, and it was bleeding through a lot of other things that led me to a really dark place.ГўВҖВқ

ГўВҖВңI think if you donГўВҖВҷt take a break and reset,ГўВҖВқ her father told her, ГўВҖВңthen youГўВҖВҷre going to stop liking surfing, and even get bitter at it.ГўВҖВқ

She kept pushing, kept surfing, not wanting to let anyone down. ГўВҖВңTrying,ГўВҖВқ Darren says, ГўВҖВңto be what people wanted her to be instead of what she was.ГўВҖВқ

The 2022 season opened in January at Pipeline on Hawai'iГўВҖВҷs North Shore, where sharp coral reefs wait just below massive, fast-crashing waves. Marks had no feel for the ocean anymore. ГўВҖВңShe looked like 50% of herself,ГўВҖВқ Luke says.

ГўВҖВңI was just numb to everything,ГўВҖВқ she says. ГўВҖВңI felt like just a shell of myself. Like, barely existing.ГўВҖВқ

After Pipe, Caroline took her familyГўВҖВҷs advice and left the tour. ГўВҖВңSometimes they know me better than I know myself,ГўВҖВқ she says. ГўВҖВңI needed to completely let go, and not think about anything else besides working on myself.ГўВҖВқ

Marks went to stay with her parents in Florida. ГўВҖВңI was really hard on myself,ГўВҖВқ she says. ГўВҖВңI felt ashamed for even needing a break.ГўВҖВқ

Her family told her not to. Even if she wanted to quit surfing for good, theyГўВҖВҷd love her the same. ГўВҖВңYou should be very proud,ГўВҖВқ Darren told her, ГўВҖВңthat you were big enough of a human beingГўВҖВ”strong enough of a womanГўВҖВ”to say,I need this.ГўВҖВқ

She rested, maybe for the first time since she was 11 years old. ГўВҖВңI was under so much pressure at such a young age for so long,ГўВҖВқ she says. ГўВҖВңIt was probably always going to happen.ГўВҖВқ

Doing deep work with her psychologist, Marks realized, ГўВҖВңI was dealing with depression, 100 percent,ГўВҖВқ she says. ГўВҖВңItГўВҖВҷs f---ing gnarly. ГўВҖВҰ In that dark place, your brain is not functioning properly. And your bodyГўВҖВҷs like, whatГўВҖВҷs going on?ГўВҖВқ

She quit Instagram, TikTok, Facebook. Researchers have well-documented evidence of social mediaГўВҖВҷs detrimental effect on teensГўВҖВҷ mental healthГўВҖВ”MetaГўВҖВҷs own studies show teenage girls who struggle with body image issues feel worse after using Instagram. Marks learned, ГўВҖВңWhen people are saying mean things, a lot of times itГўВҖВҷs coming from a deeper place that isnГўВҖВҷt even really about the way you lookГўВҖВ”itГўВҖВҷs about something to do with them.ГўВҖВқ Coupled with that, she also learned that sheГўВҖВҷs a people pleaser. ГўВҖВңIГўВҖВҷm always wanting to make sure everyoneГўВҖВҷs good and happy,ГўВҖВқ she says. ГўВҖВңBut itГўВҖВҷs impossible to please everyone.ГўВҖВқ

So she quit trying to. ГўВҖВңI just took care of me,ГўВҖВқ she says. ГўВҖВңIГўВҖВҷm taking care of me, and honestly doing the best I can do, then what other people say doesnГўВҖВҷt matter.ГўВҖВқ

Caroline went fishing with her brothers. She got a Goldendoodle puppy named Axel. ГўВҖВңI just tried to have her focus on having fun,ГўВҖВқ Luke says. ГўВҖВңAnd kinda put the whole tour scene out of her head for a bit.ГўВҖВқ

It worked. ГўВҖВңIn California,ГўВҖВқ she says, ГўВҖВңI can go train and the waves are always good, but thereГўВҖВҷs always people around, and thereГўВҖВҷs always someone you compete against out in the water, and itГўВҖВҷs kind of hard to turn your mind off. ГўВҖВҰ ThereГўВҖВҷs just something about being home in Florida that feels so grounded.ГўВҖВқ

And she went surfing with her family, right back where she caught her first waves. ГўВҖВңIt was so nice going surfing just, behind the house, with nobody else around,ГўВҖВқ she says. ГўВҖВңNobody taking pictures. Nobody else trying to surf with me. Just how we did it growing up.ГўВҖВқ

After five months away, Marks returned to the tour in June 2022. She kept social media off her phone for another few months; her team posted to her accounts.

She cruised through the end of 2022, finishing in the top five in all four events she entered, and opened ГўВҖВҷ23 by placing ninth at Pipeline on Hawai'iГўВҖВҷs North Shore. Then came the Sunset competition, with the bleeding ears, the doctor ordering surgery. ГўВҖВңI shouldГўВҖВҷve seen it coming,ГўВҖВқ Marks says. SheГўВҖВҷs suffered sinus and ear infections most of her life. Three years prior, another doctor recommended surgery, to avoid, well, this. But same as she trusted that she needed to step away beforeГўВҖВ”now she trusted that she should keep going.

USADA, which sets anti-doping rules for all Olympic and Paralympic sports, prohibits prescription painkillers and antihistamines, leaving Marks to manage her symptoms and pain with over-the-counter medication and holistic treatment. Those methods didnГўВҖВҷt do much. Nevertheless, she finished the Sunset event by placing second on her 21st birthday, telling reporters she just had a minor ear infectionГўВҖВ”but she was suffering. Throughout the season she postponed multiple flights, spent entire days in bed and went virtually deaf. ГўВҖВңThe best thing to do was to stay out of the water,ГўВҖВқ she says. ГўВҖВңBut I couldnГўВҖВҷt stay out of the water.ГўВҖВқ

In El Salvador in June, Marks notched her first win in two years. At Teahupo'o in Tahiti in August, she caught one of the best barrels of her life on one of the tourГўВҖВҷs most intimidating breaks. ГўВҖВңThat showed me,O.K., you really got this now,ГўВҖВқ she says.

Marks entered SeptemberГўВҖВҷs WSL Finals ranked No. 4 in the world. The event was back at her adopted home break, Lower Trestles. After winning the semis to qualify for the 2024 Olympics, the finals put her against none other than Carissa Moore once again, bringing her career full circle. Eight years before, she had surfed a heat against Moore at Lower Trestles in her first tour event. Four years before, she defeated Moore to win her first tour event.

This time, Marks upset Moore to win her first world title. ГўВҖВңShe just had this demeanor in the water,ГўВҖВқ Sarah says. ГўВҖВңShe carried herself with this energy that said,This is where I belong.ГўВҖВқ

One morning in early January, Marks sips coffee in the living room of her two-story San Clemente home; a hairstylist and makeup artist prep her for a photo shoot. ГўВҖВңGlammed up to put on a wetsuit and get in the ocean,ГўВҖВқ she says, laughing. ГўВҖВңI should show up like this to Pipeline. You gotta win if you do that, though. You canГўВҖВҷt do that and finish, like, third.ГўВҖВқ

High above San Clemente, windows provide panoramic views of the Pacific Ocean, and MarksГўВҖВҷs home is filled with surfing trophies and memorabilia. On her dining table, thereГўВҖВҷs the World Surf League championship trophy, in all its 75-pound glory. Outside, a sauna covers half the courtyard. In the garage, a just-installed cold plunge tubГўВҖВ”complete with purple rubber duckyГўВҖВ”sits in front of three electric bikes, beneath an array of surf jerseys. Fifty-plus surfboards lean against the opposite wall.

After loading her SUV to make for Dana Point, Marks sits in the vehicle for a while to snap selfies. SheГўВҖВҷs back on Instagram now, and working on a post. ГўВҖВң ГўВҖВҳPOV IГўВҖВҷm going surfingГўВҖВҷ?ГўВҖВқ she asks. ГўВҖВңOr, ГўВҖВҳPOV going surfing?ГўВҖВҷ ГўВҖВқ She laughs and shakes her head. ГўВҖВңThese filters are so crazy. I donГўВҖВҷt even look real.ГўВҖВқ Without posting anything, she closes the app and drives.

Naturally, sheГўВҖВҷs intent this year on winning gold in the Olympics, and then defending her world title. But she has another goal thatГўВҖВҷs proved even more elusive for her until recently. ГўВҖВңIГўВҖВҷm trying to really enjoy the moment and be present as much as I can,ГўВҖВқ she says. ГўВҖВңAnd not worry too much about the future, or dwell anymore on things in the past, and just stay focused on what IГўВҖВҷm doing right now. The only thing I can control is being a better person. ГўВҖВҰ And justlive.ГўВҖВқ

Over dinner at NickГўВҖВҷs Restaurant in San Clemente, where she ate kale and quinoa salad with steak three straight nights before the world title, Marks and some friends polish off two bottles of cabernet and gush about Caroline finally allowing herself to just live. She went on safari in South Africa, tried amazing restaurants around the world, and even went clubbing with them in Los Angeles. ГўВҖВңThis past year was probably the most fun IГўВҖВҷve ever let myself have,ГўВҖВқ Marks says. ГўВҖВңMaking memories outside of surfing. And it was so cool to see that translate to success in surfing.ГўВҖВқ

Because hereГўВҖВҷs the thing about her best moments surfing, like that barrel at Teahupo'o. ГўВҖВңI donГўВҖВҷt even remember what happened,ГўВҖВқ she says. ГўВҖВңI just donГўВҖВҷt even remember dropping in on the wave. I donГўВҖВҷt remember being in the barrel. I donГўВҖВҷt remember taking off on the wave. I kind of blacked out. ItГўВҖВҷs pretty crazy.ГўВҖВқ Afterward, she says, ГўВҖВңThe joy you get is so awesome. But I never really remember what happened.ГўВҖВқ

Dessert arrivedГўВҖВ”the house delicacy, butter cake, presented to Caroline with a candle. Everyone sang ГўВҖВңHappy BirthdayГўВҖВқ; it wasnГўВҖВҷt her birthday. She laughed so hard that her face turned red. Then she dug in.

Later, she publishes her Instagram post. ГўВҖВңPOV going surfing.ГўВҖВқ No filter.ГӮВ  ГӮВ  ГӮ

title: Taming the Wave
description: Surfing always came easy for Caroline Marks. At 22, she already has a world title and is headed to her second Olympics. But what sheГўВҖВҷs learned out of the water is the key to her long-term success and happiness
